rewritten_requirements: The system shall schedule and assign a flight number when a new flight is created.
The system shall change the flight status to 'on journey' once it takes off.
The system shall mark the flight as inactive and record it as a past record upon landing.
The system shall record cancelled flights in the same manner as landed flights if they are cancelled before starting.
The system shall remove inactive flights over time.

source_extracted_requirements.requirement: When a flight is created, it should be scheduled and assigned a flight number.
source_extracted_requirements.evidence_quote: When a flight is first created, it is scheduled and given a flight number.

source_extracted_requirements.requirement: Once the flight takes off, its status should change to 'on journey'.
source_extracted_requirements.evidence_quote: Once the flight takes off, it is simply considered to be on its journey. It stays in that phase until it reaches its destination.

source_extracted_requirements.requirement: Upon landing, the flight's status should change to inactive and be recorded as a past record.
source_extracted_requirements.evidence_quote: After landing, the flight is no longer active and becomes part of past records.

source_extracted_requirements.requirement: If a flight is cancelled before it starts, its information should still be recorded in the same manner as a landed flight.
source_extracted_requirements.evidence_quote: Even if a flight is cancelled before it starts, it is still recorded in the same way.

source_extracted_requirements.requirement: Over time, inactive flights should be removed from the system.
source_extracted_requirements.evidence_quote: Over time, when the flight is no longer important, its information is removed from the system.
